background.experience: shift handoffs, maintenance release records, and fault isolation
background.attention_bias: sequence of authorization, execution, discrepancy, and return-to-service
background.counter_bias: do not treat a successful command as proof that the whole system is airworthy

readers.thesis: The owned worker becomes credible when every build follows an explicit handoff from source identity through execution to a verifiable release receipt.
observations: The user repeatedly converts missing hosted capability into a demand for owned execution rather than a policy exception.
The implementation preserves that sequence by binding job identity, command execution, output inventory, and receipt digest.
uncertainties: Complete private conversation history and all prior private reader notes were unavailable to this run.

background.experience: batch schedulers, reproducible scientific workloads, and hostile shared-cluster failure modes
background.attention_bias: environment closure, resource boundaries, provenance, and nondeterministic state
background.counter_bias: do not mistake environment normalization for kernel-level isolation

readers.thesis: The worker establishes a deterministic execution contract but remains non-sovereign against untrusted jobs until resource, network, filesystem, and kernel isolation become enforceable gates.
observations: The artifact closes locale, time, and hosted-provider identity inputs while content-addressing declared outputs.
The threat boundary remains process-level because the prototype does not isolate network, CPU, memory, syscalls, or the host filesystem.
uncertainties: Cross-platform mode normalization and containerized execution remain unverified.

agreements: Build authority resides in the job contract, owned worker, and output receipt rather than the hosted runner brand.
disagreements: The chronological reader treats authorization handoff as the primary risk; the semantic reader treats host isolation as the primary risk.
omissions_detected: The prototype lacks signed job admission, network denial, resource quotas, and a remote worker enrollment protocol.
revisions_after_discussion: The next worker version binds signed admission to an isolated runtime and preserves the current deterministic receipt format.

notes_added: A worker receipt links source identity, normalized execution, and output custody without granting authority to the machine provider.
Process-level determinism and host-level isolation remain separate capabilities and receive separate verification gates.
notes_challenged: A normalized environment alone does not prove a clean or secure build boundary.

privacy.review: Contains only technical abstractions, synthetic capability statements, repository facts, and explicit coverage limits; excludes private transcript text and personal facts.
